description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:24 image with a docker:24-dind service. Define stages: build, test, review, staging, production, dast, performance. In the build stage add a build job that logs into $CI_REGISTRY, runs docker build with proxy build‑args and tags $CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG, then docker push the image. In the test stage add a test job that sequentially runs container_scanning (docker run arminc/clair-db, download clair‑scanner, wait for daemon, execute scanner producing gl-container-scanning-report.json), code_quality (docker run the GitLab codequality image), license_management (/run.sh analyze .), sast (docker run the GitLab SAST image), and dependency_scanning (docker run the GitLab dependency‑scanning image), publishing the generated JSON reports as artifacts. In the review stage add a review job that calls the deploy function with track=review, which ensures the namespace, creates a registry secret, runs helm upgrade --install using the chart/, sets image repository and tag, and waits for the rollout; the job runs only for merge‑request pipelines. In the staging stage add a staging job that calls deploy with track=stable and runs only on the main branch, and in the production stage add a production job that calls deploy with track=stable and runs only when a tag is pushed. Add a dast job that runs the dast function (sets CI_ENVIRONMENT_URL, runs /zap/zap‑baseline.py against the URL, saves gl-dast-report.json) and a performance job that runs the performance function (downloads gitlab‑exporter, runs sitespeed.io, saves sitespeed-results) – both publish their reports as artifacts.
